subject: BPF_HASH macro
new: explicit struct with __uint(type, BPF_MAP_TYPE_HASH) annotated SEC(".maps")
truth: Stop using BPF_HASH macro; define an explicit struct with __uint(type, BPF_MAP_TYPE_HASH) and SEC(".maps") annotation instead.
quote: The `BPF_HASH(name, ktype, vtype)` map macro is replaced by an explicit struct with `__uint(type, BPF_MAP_TYPE_HASH)` annotated `SEC(".maps")`.

from_fact: explicit struct with __uint(type, bpf_map_type_hash) annotated sec(".maps")

subject: BPF_ARRAY macro
truth: Stop using BPF_ARRAY macro; define an explicit BPF_MAP_TYPE_ARRAY struct with SEC(".maps") annotation instead.
quote: The `BPF_ARRAY(name, type, size)` macro is replaced by an explicit `BPF_MAP_TYPE_ARRAY` struct annotated `SEC(".maps")`.

subject: BPF_PERF_OUTPUT macro
truth: Stop using BPF_PERF_OUTPUT macro; define an explicit BPF_MAP_TYPE_PERF_EVENT_ARRAY struct with SEC(".maps") annotation instead.
quote: The `BPF_PERF_OUTPUT(name)` macro is replaced by an explicit `BPF_MAP_TYPE_PERF_EVENT_ARRAY` struct annotated `SEC(".maps")`.

subject: map.lookup method
truth: Stop using map.lookup(&key); call bpf_map_lookup_elem(&map, &key) instead.

subject: map.update method
truth: Stop using map.update(&key, &val); call bpf_map_update_elem(&map, &key, &val, 0) instead.

subject: events.perf_submit method
truth: Stop using events.perf_submit(ctx, data, len); call bpf_perf_event_output(ctx, &events, BPF_F_CURRENT_CPU, data, len) instead.
quote: The `events.perf_submit(ctx, data, len)` method is replaced by `bpf_perf_event_output(ctx, &events, BPF_F_CURRENT_CPU, data, len)`.

subject: kernel header includes
truth: Stop relying on BCC's implicit kernel headers; include a single #include "vmlinux.h" instead.
quote: BCC's implicit `#include <linux/...>` kernel headers are replaced by a single `#include "vmlinux.h"`.

subject: required includes for libbpf
old: BCC needed no explicit includes for helpers/core_read
truth: You must now explicitly #include <bpf/bpf_helpers.h> and #include <bpf/bpf_core_read.h> in every BPF program.
quote: libbpf requires explicit `#include <bpf/bpf_helpers.h>` and `#include <bpf/bpf_core_read.h>` (BCC needed none).

from_fact: bcc needed no explicit includes for helpers/core_read

subject: kernel struct field access
truth: Stop using direct kernel struct field access ptr->field->subfield; use BPF_CORE_READ(ptr, field, subfield) instead.
quote: Direct kernel struct field access `ptr->field->subfield` is replaced by `BPF_CORE_READ(ptr, field, subfield)`.

subject: kprobe function definition
truth: Stop using int kprobe__func(struct pt_regs *ctx, args); use SEC("kprobe/func") int BPF_KPROBE(kprobe__func, args) instead.
quote: The BCC kprobe form `int kprobe__func(struct pt_regs *ctx, args)` is replaced by `SEC("kprobe/func") int BPF_KPROBE(kprobe__func, args)`.

subject: section naming
new: explicit SEC("...") annotation on every program
truth: You must now add an explicit SEC("...") annotation on every BPF program instead of relying on BCC's implicit naming.
quote: BCC's implicit section naming is replaced by an explicit `SEC("...")` annotation on every program.

from_fact: explicit sec("...") annotation on every program

subject: compile-time config substitution
truth: Stop using #define for config values in BPF programs; use global const volatile variables instead.
quote: BCC's compile-time `#define` config substitution is replaced by global `const volatile` variables.

subject: runtime compilation approach
new: generated skeleton loaded via name__open(), name__load(), name__attach()
truth: Stop using BCC's runtime compilation; use a generated skeleton with name__open(), name__load(), and name__attach() instead.
quote: BCC runtime compilation is replaced by a generated skeleton loaded via `name__open()`, `name__load()`, and `name__attach()`.

from_fact: generated skeleton loaded via name__open(), name__load(), name__attach()

subject: reading global variables from userspace
truth: Read global variables from userspace via skel->rodata, skel->bss, or skel->data instead of BCC's previous approach.
quote: User space reads global variables via `skel->rodata`, `skel->bss`, or `skel->data`.

subject: bpf_trace_printk
truth: Stop using bpf_trace_printk(); use bpf_printk() instead, which has a 3-argument limit.

subject: static helper functions
truth: All static helper functions in BPF programs must now be marked static __always_inline.
quote: Static helper functions must be marked `static __always_inline`.
